# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

```yaml
stages:  # Define your stage names (test, build, etc.) here as they will be used in subsequent sections of this file. You can add more if you want to include them later on or create new ones for specific tasks like 'deploy' and so forth...
- test
- build
- deploy

variables:  # Define your variables that are going to change across the stages here (like database URLs, etc.) as they will be used in subsequent sections of this file. You can add more if you want or create new ones for specific tasks like 'db_url' and so forth...
- db_url=database://localhost:3051/myapp?user=root&password=secret#mysql

test:  # Define the test stage here (where we run our tests) as it will be used in subsequent sections of this file. You can add more if you want to include them later on or create new ones for specific tasks like 'db_setup' and so forth...
- python==3.8.* pip install --upgrade pytest flask gunicorn psycopg2-binary # Add your test dependencies here (if any) as they will be used in subsequent sections of this file..  You can use the latest versions if you want to automatically get them using 'pip list' command
+ python==3.8.* pip install -r requirements/test.txt --no-cache-dir # Add your test dependencies here (if any) as they will be used in subsequent sections of this file..  You can use the latest versions if you want to automatically get them using 'pip list' command
+ pytest tests/*.py   # Run all python files inside "tests" directory with PyTest framework, add more commands based on your requirements... (e.g., adding coverage report)
- flask run --host=0.0.0.0  & gunicorn -w 3 main:app    # Add any other command lines here that you want to include in the test stage as they will be used at this point, if needed... (e.g., adding a specific port for Flask or Gunicorn)
- python==3.* pip list --outdated   # List outdated packages and upgrade them automatically using 'pip install -U' command  If you want to use the latest versions of all dependencies instead then remove this line..(You can add more if needed...)
+ gunicorn main:app    # Add any other commands lines here that are specific for deploy stage, like starting a production server... (e.g., adding an environment variable)  If you want to use the latest versions of all dependencies instead then remove this line..(You can add more if needed...)
